lifeos_release_catalog_rows.notes: LifeOS desktop app bundle for the local Ubuntu release lane (defaults to deb).

flexnetos_agents.text: This is the first file to read when working under `/home/flexnetos/FlexNetOS`.
The workspace root is a hollow orchestration area, not a monorepo. Real
implementation work belongs in independent peer repositories under `src/`.
Yazelix and meta are both organization-owned peer repos in this workspace. Do
not place them under `src/upstream/` unless creating a separate comparison clone.

flexnetos_layout.text: This is a hollow workspace for the fresh `flexnetos` user. The workspace root is not a monorepo, vendor directory, or submodule container.
External upstream peer repos, when needed for inspection or pinned source evidence:
| `/home/flexnetos/FlexNetOS/var/lib/gitkb` | GitKB local state target after GitKB install/init. |

lifeos_cargo.text: #  - src-tauri/        — Tauri 2 desktop shell (binary + lifeos_lib staticlib/cdylib/rlib)
#  - crates/lifeos-core — portable Rust core (types, auth, MCP clients, sync protocols)
#                        own rust-toolchain.toml. Lives as a standalone sibling project,
# The workspace exists to share the future `lifeos-daemon` bin crate and `lifeos-core`
# across the desktop shell + headless Pi daemon without duplicating dependencies. The
# Tauri shell continues to be invoked via `cargo tauri dev` / `cargo tauri build` from
# the `src-tauri/` directory; cargo resolves up to this manifest as the workspace root.

lifeos_package.text: "description": "LifeOS — Vue 3 + Tauri UI kit (Path B). Sibling of ui_kits/lifeos_app/ React canon.",

lifeos_readme.text: > **LifeOS — the AI agent that runs your home and your life.**
> Work, personal, and home automation in one operating system, with the assistant baked into every surface.

Cross-platform desktop app built on **Vue 3 + Vite + Pinia + vue-router** with a **Tauri 2** native shell. Targets Linux / macOS / Windows desktop, with a web build that runs in Firefox / Chrome / Edge / mobile browsers.

This repo is the **production implementation** of the [LifeOS Design System](./design-system-reference/README.md) handoff bundle. The bundle's authoritative tokens, fonts, brand assets, and component-level CSS are folded into this repo so it is fully self-contained.

release_catalog_lifeos.text: lifeos	bun-tauri	src/lifeos	src/lifeos/package.json	lifeos	lifeos-bundle	LifeOS desktop app bundle for the local Ubuntu release lane (defaults to deb).

conclusion.answer_to_question: The artifacts show FlexNetOS was used for the operating/control-plane work that lifeos did not own: repo orchestration, runtime configuration, environment state, releases, local proof capture, and migration artifact generation. lifeos was consumed by that system as an application artifact.
flexnetos_usage: Workspace root and peer-repo coordinator for the source fleet.
Meta/gitkb control plane and policy surface.
Envctl environment authority and state/catalog contract.
Runner/release/provenance lane that packages tools and product bundles.
Yazelix/Codex/local runtime proof surface and migration evidence sink.
lifeos_usage: Vue 3/Vite/Tauri desktop and web product app.
Release catalog entry packaged through the FlexNetOS runner lane as a bun-tauri bundle.
Planning-spine state/proof work inside its own repo branch.
Future Rust workspace for lifeos-core, lifeos-daemon, and Tauri shell work.
conclusion.summary: FlexNetOS was used as the active hollow workspace, control plane, environment authority, runtime/release lane, and migration artifact sink. lifeos was present as a peer Vue/Tauri product app under src/lifeos and as a release catalog bundle, not as the active filesystem root or control-plane service.

history_and_state_evidence.memory_context: Prior roadmap work treated lifeos as a portable release target under the FlexNetOS workspace, while the live scan here verifies the current files before using that context.

input_packet.goal: Determine what FlexNetOS was used for instead of lifeos through artifacts, history, code maps, and dependency scans
